The quarter-end market closing price is utilized to calculate the Share Value.
Messages
Notice of T+1 Settlement Cycle Transition
Effective May 28, 2024, Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) rules will require
that most U.S. securities transactions settle on or before the next business day
following the trade date (a settlement date of T+1).
For additional details about T+1 settlement and eligible securities, please visit the
Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) website at www.dtcc.com/ust1. You
can also view the full text of the final rules on the SEC website at www.sec.gov.

. SIPC Protection
Availability of Free Credit Balances and Financial Statements We are a member of Securities Investor Protection Corporation
Under the customer protection rules of the SEC [17 CFR (SIPC), which protects securities of its customers up to $500,000
§240.15c3-3], we may use funds comprising free credit balances (including $250,000 for claims for cash). An explanatory brochure
carried for customer accounts here, provided that these funds are is available upon request or at www.sipc.org. Losses due to market
payable to customers on demand (i.e., are free of a lien or right of fluctuation are not protected by SIPC and assets not held with us
set-off in our favor or on behalf of some third party to whom you may not be covered by SIPC protection. To obtain information about
